title: Cognitive Load Tracker
tagline: Bridging HCI research and real-time software instrumentation
description: A research tool that correlates task-switching behavior with self-reported cognitive load, combining browser telemetry with validated psychometric scales.
problem: Cognitive load is hard to measure in situ. Existing lab studies lack ecological validity; field studies lack structured instrumentation.
approach: Built a browser extension that logs focus events and tab switches, paired with periodic NASA-TLX micro-surveys. A Python backend aggregates sessions and visualizes load curves over time.
solution: Pilot study with 12 participants showed a significant correlation (r ≈ 0.72) between switch frequency and reported mental demand during coding tasks.
learned: The intersection of CS and CogSci is most powerful when instrumentation is lightweight and consent-first. Small n is fine for exploratory research if the pipeline is reproducible.
